channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Trump signs executive order to study how to expand IVF
item.description: Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order meant to expand access to and reduce costs of in vitro fertilization. He also issued a presidential memorandum calling for “radical transparency requirements” from the government, which he suggested could reduce wasteful spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump 'lives' in Russian 'disinformation space': Zelenskyy
item.description: Zelenskyy's claim follows Trump's news conference Tuesday evening, where he criticized the Ukrainian leader.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Dramatic dashcam footage shows moment Sacramento driver flies off busy overpass: video
item.description: Dashcam footage captured the dramatic moment when a vehicle flew off the Interstate 5 overpass and dropped on to a busy Sacramento freeway on Presidents' Day, injuring only the driver.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winter's next wave of storms takes aim at the East Coast
item.description: The latest in a long line of winter storms is taking direct aim at the East Coast, threatening to dump heavy snow and some ice in several states.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brutal polar vortex cold shutters Midwest schools, breaks North Dakota record
item.description: More than 95 million people are facing gripping cold Tuesday as a polar vortex sends temperatures plunging to record levels, closing schools, bursting pipes and forcing communities to set up more temporary shelters for the homeless.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Southwest cutting nearly 1,800 jobs, airline says
item.description: Those losing their jobs are reportedly in the airline’s corporate offices, including some 11 senior positions with titles of vice president or higher, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's firing of 1,000 national park workers raises concerns about maintenance and operating hours
item.description: The Trump administration has fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees who maintain and clean parks, educate visitors and perform other functions as part of its broad-based effort to downsize government.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Fertility clinic sued after giving woman the wrong IVF baby
item.description: A former patient of Coastal Fertility Specialists has filed a lawsuit against the company for implanting the wrong embryo into her uterus, resulting in her carrying and giving birth to another couple’s biological child.
item.dc:creator: Dylan James, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: SUMTERVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Native American activist Leonard Peltier was released from a Florida prison on Tuesday, weeks after then-President Joe Biden angered law enforcement officials by commuting his life sentence to home confinement in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chutkan refuses to block Musk, DOGE from 7 federal agencies
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan on Tuesday refused the request of 14 Democratic state attorneys general to immediately impose wide-ranging restrictions on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows ICE preparing illegal immigrants to be flown out of Seattle airport
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, or ICE, prepared a group of illegal undocumented immigrants to be flown back to their home countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: NYC voters would likely support Gov. Kathy Hochul removing Adams as mayor, AP VoteCast shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As New York Gov. Kathy Hochul weighs whether to remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, AP VoteCast shows that many New York City voters in last year's election were already on board with the move.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alleged 'Zizians' leader was once declared dead in California
item.description: LaSota was still alive, however, when state police officers found and arrested her Sunday in Allegany County, Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Immigrants could be in US a decade before their case is resolved, lawyers say
item.description: The recent firing of 20 U.S. immigration judges will add to delays for an already overwhelmed immigration system, lawyers tell Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KTLA 5

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, KTLA 5

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multiple killings in the US
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The apparent leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians has been arrested in Maryland along with another member of the group, Maryland State Police said Monday. Jack Lasota, 34, was arrested Sunday along with Michelle Zajko, 33, of Media, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eagles player declines actor’s offer to pay Super Bowl fine
item.description: Actor and Eagles fan Rob McElhenny, best known for co-creating and starring in FXX’s “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia,” had recently offered to foot the bill for a fine incurred by Eagles player Jalyx Hunt during Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio dies at 77
item.description: Legendary Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio has passed away at 77 years old. Paquita, whose real name is Francisca Viveros Barradas, passed away at her home in Veracruz, Mexico. Her family revealed her passing in a post on Monday morning on Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: As Trump administration reforms the EPA, cleanups of America's most toxic sites are uncertain
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just over a mile from where Patricia Flores has lived for almost 20 years, a battery smelter plant spewed toxic elements into the environment for nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4
